The only point to consider is that source map URLs embedded in files are standard but can sometimes be exploited if they point to malicious sources or if source maps contain sensitive information. Here, they are base64-encoded JSON referencing the same file, which is typical.\n\n**Final recommendations:**\n- The scores are reasonable and consistent.\n- No changes needed.\n- The overall risk is effectively zero, and malware or obfuscation are absent.\n\n---\n\n### **Overall conclusion:**\n- **Malware score:** 0\n- **Obfuscated score:** 0\n- **Risk score:** 0\n\nThese files are benign, autogenerated source map or declaration files with no security concerns.",
